req.title: Open checking account (boundary variant)
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-026 defines a closed numeric range [25, 50000] for initial deposits with two actor states (verified / unverified). Test design applies: EP to partition valid mid-range and invalid out-of-range deposits; BVA to probe exact boundary values (25, 50000) and their immediate neighbours (24, 50001); NEG for the unverified-customer 403 path; DT to combine actor state with deposit validity; STC to confirm the account transitions from non-existent to active on success. The violation in the original response was test[4] (above-maximum deposit rejected) which used kind=negative but heuristic=BVA — a logical inconsistency because BVA boundary probes that fall outside the valid range and expect rejection should be classified as kind=boundary, not negative. Fix: change test[4] kind from 'negative' to 'boundary', which correctly reflects that the value 50001 is a boundary probe (one unit above the upper boundary) exercising boundary behaviour as required by the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_open_checking_mid_range_deposit_verified_customer
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
trace: R-FIN-026: valid deposit in mid-range partition
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_checking_mid_range_deposit_verified_customer(
    verified_customer_token, api_client, assert_notification_sent
):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 1000}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert_notification_sent(body["account_id"], notification_type="welcome")

tests.name: test_open_checking_below_minimum_deposit_rejected
preconditions: deposit = 24 (one unit below minimum, invalid partition)
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains error code indicating deposit below minimum
trace: R-FIN-026: account must NOT be created on invalid deposit

tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_open_checking_at_exact_maximum_boundary_deposit(
    verified_customer_token, api_client, assert_notification_sent
):
    payload = {"initial_deposit": 50000}
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/accounts/checking",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {verified_customer_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "account_id" in body
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert_notification_sent(body["account_id"], notification_type="welcome")

tests.name: test_open_checking_above_maximum_deposit_rejected
preconditions: deposit = 50001 (one unit above maximum boundary, outside valid range)
actions: Obtain auth token for a verified customer via <fixture: verified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains error indicating deposit above maximum
trace: R-FIN-026: boundary behaviour just above max tested
R-FIN-026: BVA probe at upper boundary + 1 confirms range enforcement

tests.name: test_open_checking_unverified_customer_forbidden
preconditions: deposit = 1000 (valid range, to isolate actor-state variable)
actions: Obtain auth token for an unverified customer via <fixture: unverified_customer_token>
expected: Response body contains error indicating customer not verified
trace: R-FIN-026: 403 error pathway when customer.verified = false
